validation_outcome: Repo-root `make` builds cleanly (exit 0) with no new warnings in FGame.cpp/FGame.h. The test build (`cd tests && make`) links successfully and the top-level suite (`./SSWTests` run with cwd = tests/) reports OK (210 tests), all passing. The wxWidgets 3.3.2 + CppUnit toolchain is present in this environment, so both required validation commands ran to completion in the foreground. The fix swaps the case-4 and case-5 bodies in checkForVictory()'s switch(m_satharRetreat): case 4 now evaluates the STATIONS condition (m_stationsDestroyed < 2 at a tenday boundary, resetting m_stationsDestroyed = 0) and case 5 the SHIP-LOSS condition (m_lostTendaySathar > m_lostTendayUPF at a tenday boundary, resetting m_lostTendaySathar = 0 and m_lostTendayUPF = 0), matching showRetreatConditions() text. The tenday gate (m_round % 10 == 0 && m_round > 0) is preserved on both cases; cases 1-3, the station-count Sathar-victory scan, and fighter/militia (S2/S3) counting are unchanged. The Doxygen header on checkForVictory() was updated (description documents the per-case condition mapping and the C2 swap fix; @date Last Modified Jul 11, 2026; @author 'Tom Stephens, Claude Sonnet 5 (medium)'). NOTE for Tester: checkForVictory() is private (reached via public FGame::endUPFTurn()), and m_satharRetreat / m_stationsDestroyed / m_lostTendaySathar / m_lostTendayUPF / m_round are private with no public setters today; a minimal implementer-provided test seam may be required to drive the case-4/case-5 branches with controlled inputs -- see tester_prompt.txt.
